verdicts.note: Defensible hard-asset MPC land NAV; insider (Pershing Square) transacting at $100 anchors floor ~26% above price; Ackman Berkshire-style holdco catalyst. But heavy leverage ($5.8B debt +$1B pref), decade-slow land realization, pivot deploys into insurance not realization. Strongest of the four.
Active winddown but soft floor — 77% of equity is levered Safehold stock + illiquid Asbury Park land; conflicted external manager (8% SAFE loan +$55M termination fee + fee load) eats NAV; open-ended.

Real equity cushion (assets >> $50M debt +$70M pref) but liquidation STALLED — zero common distributions in 4yr, marquee Dallas sale slips to ~2028, July-31 term-loan cliff + going-concern doubt, serial impairments melt the floor, shareholder revolt.

Catalyst already FIRED — 59->18 properties, sales +34% above book, ~$15/sh specials paid, termination authority approved, near-zero leverage. Value harvested; residual stub ~at book on low-quality office dregs.

takeaway: The asset_revaluation lens finds REAL hidden-asset discounts (all 4 have hard-asset floors + 25-40% NAV discounts, more real than the melting cash-boxes), but the realization CATALYST is the gate and none cleared cleanly: harvested (NLOP), stalled (SRG), soft/conflicted (STHO), leverage+slow (HHH). 0 clean FUND, 4 constructive WATCH. HHH the best (insider-anchored NAV + compounding catalyst). Lens validated as a candidate source; clean-FUND base rate stays low.
